title: ready/blocked derivation ignores the *_gated_by_item gating relations; only task_depends_on_task is honored
description: currentState (context-sdk.ts:682), the sole ready/blocked/next deriver, computes blocked + nextActions ordering only from task_depends_on_task (depParentsOf, context-sdk.ts:727-730). It ignores the gating relations task_gated_by_item / feature_gated_by_item / story_gated_by_item, so a task gated by an incomplete item is reported ready. feature_gated_by_item / story_gated_by_item have no readiness deriver at all (currentState buckets only the tasks block). roadmap-plan.ts orders phases by phase_depends_on (no phase_gated_by_item; out of scope).

evidence.reference: depParentsOf filters only task_depends_on_task; feeds blocked (:742) + topoSort order (:787)
currentState is the sole ready/blocked deriver; buckets only the tasks block — no feature/story readiness derivation exists
context-current-state op description promises ranked unblocked/blocked tasks — wrong while gates are ignored

impact: Orderings expressed as gates (task_gated_by_item; feature/story_gated_by_item) are invisible to 'what is ready': a gated task surfaces as a next-action, and feature/story gates have no derivation. Cross-arc orderings recorded as gates are documentation-only (carried in a tracker md), not derived.
proposed_resolution: NOW (task level): extend currentState's depParentsOf (context-sdk.ts:727-730) to also fold in task_gated_by_item parents, treating a task gated by an incomplete/unclosed item as blocked (resolving non-task gate targets to a complete/closed bucket), so blocked + the nextActions topo order honor gates. FORWARD (the proper home, FEAT-004): FEAT-004's config-declared blocked-by relation_type must be a SET including the gating relations (task_/feature_/story_gated_by_item) alongside the *_depends_on relations, and FEAT-004 must add feature/story-level readiness derivation — so gate-awareness is config-declared and covers all kinds, not hardcoded to tasks. Until FEAT-004, the currentState extension closes only the task-level case.
